_comment: Project-level Claude Code settings shipped with the template. Three hooks enforce the session-durability habits: (1) Stop blocks if HANDOFF.md is stale, forcing a resume-snapshot every session; (2) Stop archives the raw session jsonl into .session-archive/ so full-fidelity history survives; (3) PreCompact mirrors the archive and asks Claude to refresh HANDOFF.md before auto-compaction summarizes the conversation away. Delete any hook you don't want, but at least keep the HANDOFF one — it is the single most load-bearing habit in this template.

hooks.command: HANDOFF="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}/HANDOFF.md"; if [ -f "$HANDOFF" ]; then mtime=$(stat -c %Y "$HANDOFF" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m "$HANDOFF" 2>/dev/null); age=$(( $(date +%s) - mtime )); [ "$age" -lt 1800 ] && exit 0; fi; printf '%s\n' "{\"decision\":\"block\",\"reason\":\"Before ending this session, write or update HANDOFF.md at $HANDOFF so work can be resumed if the session is killed. Include these sections: ## Current Task, ## Files Touched (path + role), ## Done (completed this session), ## In Progress (active edits, partial thoughts, hypotheses to test), ## Next Step (the exact next action). Overwrite the whole file each time — it is a snapshot, not a log. After writing, stop normally; the hook will see the fresh mtime and let you stop.\"}"

INPUT=$(cat); SID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r .session_id 2>/dev/null); PROJ_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"; SLUG=$(echo "$PROJ_DIR" | tr / -); SRC="$HOME/.claude/projects/$SLUG/$SID.jsonl"; DST_DIR="$PROJ_DIR/.session-archive"; mkdir -p "$DST_DIR"; [ -f "$SRC" ] && cp "$SRC" "$DST_DIR/$SID.jsonl" 2>/dev/null; exit 0

INPUT=$(cat); SID=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r .session_id 2>/dev/null); PROJ_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$PWD}"; SLUG=$(echo "$PROJ_DIR" | tr / -); SRC="$HOME/.claude/projects/$SLUG/$SID.jsonl"; DST_DIR="$PROJ_DIR/.session-archive"; mkdir -p "$DST_DIR"; [ -f "$SRC" ] && cp "$SRC" "$DST_DIR/$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)-precompact-$SID.jsonl" 2>/dev/null; exit 0

hooks.prompt:
The conversation is about to be compacted. Before the compaction summary is generated, refresh ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-/workspace}/HANDOFF.md so no critical state is lost in summarization.

Read the session transcript at $HOME/.claude/projects/-workspace/<latest session-id>.jsonl (pick the jsonl file with the newest mtime in that directory). From it, extract and OVERWRITE HANDOFF.md with these sections:

## Current Task — one paragraph: what is actively being worked on and why.

## Files Touched — bullet list of (absolute path, role, version/commit if applicable).

## Done — commits pushed this session (short SHA + one-line why). Include verified outcomes like 'v2.77 stable for 4 h'.

## In Progress — hypotheses under test, specific file paths + line numbers being investigated, any secrets the user shared (name + where stored, NEVER the value), and scheduled wakeups with their trigger times.

## Next Step — single exact next action to take. If waiting on the user, state what we're waiting for.

Be terse but specific. A reader who hasn't seen the conversation must be able to resume work. Overwrite, don't append. After writing, exit.
